paper_title: Foraminiferal Assemblages and Environmental Parameters Analysis

methods.method_name: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on Foraminiferal Assemblages
methods.description: This method involves performing PCA on the relative abundances of dominant foraminiferal species to identify faunal groups and understand their relation to environmental parameters.

steps.description: Perform PCA based on the relative abundances of the dominant species (> 2 %) in the > 125 µm (0-10 cm) size fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on foraminiferal species abundance data

steps.description: Perform a second PCA on the relative abundance data of dominant species in the > 63 µm fraction (0-1 cm).
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on smaller size fraction of foraminiferal species

methods.method_name: Canonical Correspondence Analysis (CCA) on Geochemical Data
methods.description: This method uses CCA to analyze the relationship between geochemical data and the cumulative percentages of identified foraminiferal species groups.

steps.description: Perform CCA on the geochemical data (depth, BWO, OC, oxygen and nitrate penetration depth) and on the cumulative percentages of the three species groups.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA on geochemical data and species group percentages

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting sediment samples using a multicorer and preparing them for analysis.

steps.description: Sample a ten-station transect along the Murray Ridge using a multicorer during the PASOM cruise.
Slice sediment cores at specified intervals for different analyses.
